standard_variable.semantic_intent: microbiology organism branch under a specimen-test event
standard_variable.semantic_grain: one row per microbiology specimen-test-organism branch

database_mapping.source_grain: one opening-scope source organism branch under a specimen-test event before branch aggregation
database_mapping.target_grain: one retained organism branch row per microbiology_organism_branch_id

microbiology_family_build_translation.entity_grain_translation: aggregate source rows to one microbiology_organism_branch_id per parent event and organism branch identity
microbiology_family_build_translation.source_hierarchy_translation: retain organism branches as children of parent microbiology_test_event rows and parents of susceptibility leaf rows
microbiology_family_build_translation.parent_child_translation: link each organism branch to microbiology_test_event_id and expose susceptibility child presence/count
microbiology_family_build_translation.raw_text_retention_translation: retain raw organism name, isolate number, quantity text, organism comments, and branch role/positivity helper classes
microbiology_family_build_translation.no_row_translation: absence of a retained organism branch means no approved organism branch under this mapping, not definitive negative culture

validation_contract.no_row_policy_check: absence of row is not proof of negative culture or no organism; use parent event context and raw comments
notes: Not every organism branch is a true positive isolate; use organism_positive_analysis_class.
Quantity text is retained raw and should not be treated as a standardized colony count.

execution_contract.formal_output_rule: The governed execution.py entrypoint should read this mapping spec and delegate to the current local reviewed implementation.
execution_contract.non_bypassable_target_rule: Formal std_microbiology_organism_isolate outputs for MIMIC-IV-3.1 should be produced only through the governed execution.py path.
